extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_b is set true only by r1 (IF hyperkalemia), and rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (IF hyperkalemia). Both rules fire on the same condition (hyperkalemia=true) but target different outputs, so the per-output priority/tie-break resolution never puts them in conflict — each output is set independently. There is no rule that sets either output false, and the default only applies to unset outputs. Therefore with hyperkalemia=true, both r1 and r2 fire, yielding rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, making (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) true and violating mutual exclusion. elderly is irrelevant (it only affects rec_drug_c via r3); set to false. Counterexample: hyperkalemia=true, elderly=false produces rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=true, rec_drug_c=false.
